title: where should you start
description: A short survey everyone takes after picking a path. It places you into Version A (beginner) or Version B (intermediate) so the chapters meet you where you are. There are no wrong answers. Answer honestly and the course fits better.
instructions: Pick the answer closest to true for you. Honest beats impressive: a high score you can't back up just means harder chapters.

outcomes.label: Version B (intermediate)
outcomes.result: Start every chapter in Version B: faster pace, less hand-holding, more under-the-hood detail.

outcomes.label: Version A (beginner)
outcomes.result: Start every chapter in Version A: slower pace, more explanation, full hand-holding through errors.

scoring.notes: Each question is worth a maximum of 4 points; 13 questions give a maxScore of 52. The Version B threshold is 50 percent (26 points). A learner can switch versions at any time from the chapter view; this survey only sets the starting point.

questions.prompt: How often do you use AI chat tools like ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini?

options.label: I have never used one
I tried one once or twice
I use one now and then, maybe monthly
I use one most weeks
I use one most days

questions.prompt: Have you used an AI coding tool, like Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, or the Codex CLI?

options.label: I had not heard of these before now
I have heard of them but never used one
I have tried one a little
I use one regularly

questions.prompt: An AI tool gives you a block of work, text or code, and says it is finished. What do you usually do with it?

options.label: I have not gotten output from an AI tool yet
I use it as-is and trust that it is right
I skim it quickly before using it
I read it carefully, and I often catch things that are wrong

questions.prompt: An AI assistant hands you some code and says it works. What is your next move?

options.label: I would not know how to read the code
Run it and assume it is fine
Ask the AI if it is sure
Read through it myself to understand what it does before trusting it

questions.prompt: Have you written code before, in any language?

options.label: Never
I have copied and pasted snippets that other people or AI wrote
I have written small scripts with a lot of help
I have written working programs on my own
Never
A little, following tutorials step by step
Yes, and I am fairly comfortable with it

questions.prompt: In programming, what is a variable?

options.label: I am not sure
A named place that holds a value you can use and change later
A kind of error message
A math equation the computer solves

questions.prompt: You see this line of code:

options.label: I am not sure
Multiplies price by 3 and stores the result in total
Checks whether price is equal to 3
Prints price three times

questions.prompt: Have you used a terminal, also called the command line?

options.label: I have never opened one
I have opened one but was not sure what to do
I run commands in it occasionally
I am comfortable working in it

questions.prompt: In a terminal, what does the cd command do?

options.label: I am not sure
Changes which folder you are currently in
Copies a file from one place to another
Closes the terminal

questions.prompt: Read this code and predict what it prints:

options.label: I am not sure
a b c
An error

questions.prompt: Read this code and predict what it prints:

options.label: I am not sure
2, then 4, then 6, each on its own line
1, then 2, then 3, each on its own line

questions.prompt: How comfortable are you reading code that someone else, or an AI, wrote?

options.label: I cannot read code at all yet
I can guess at what simple lines do
I can follow most of it
I read it fluently
